g.text: RACE CONDITION if shutdown_flag is plain int
Compiler may hoist the read out of the loop:
Result: flag written by signal handler is never seen.
Prevents CPU and compiler reordering across the flag
T+0ms accept() returns EINTR (signal interrupts syscall)
T+0ms accept loop exits, server_fd closed
T+Xms Workers finish in-flight requests (X = longest active request)
T+Xms Clean exit — no truncated responses

text.tspan: (plain int: compiler may cache in register,
never re-reads from memory → loop spins forever)

g.text: OS stops queueing new connections immediately

text.tspan: (signal wakes only ONE — wrong for shutdown)
shutdown && count==0 is the exit condition
